name: Territory Control
description: A strategic ownership system where players or teams capture, contest, defend, and exploit map regions for resources, scoring, visibility, movement, or production.
design_notes: Territory control works when ownership changes are readable and consequential. Designers should define region boundaries, capture rules, contested state, ownership transactions, scoring hooks, late-join sync, and how territory interacts with fog, resources, and unit movement.
design_purpose: Turn map position into a measurable strategic resource.
Create conflict over regions that generate income, vision, spawn access, or victory progress.
Give level designers control over pacing through chokepoints, neutral zones, and contested objectives.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are expanding influence across the map and forcing opponents to respond.

parameters.name: territory_region_id
parameters.description: Map region or objective zone with ownership state.

parameters.name: control_owner_id
parameters.description: Current owning player, team, or faction.

parameters.name: capture_progress
parameters.description: Normalized capture completion for the current contender.

parameters.name: contested_state
parameters.description: Conflict state derived from units, structures, or scripted rules.

parameters.name: capture_source_id
parameters.description: Entity or rule currently contributing capture pressure.

parameters.name: ownership_transaction_id
parameters.description: Committed ownership change used by scoring, replay, and sync.

parameters.name: resource_income_rate
parameters.description: Income generated while the region is owned and connected.

edge_cases: Two teams enter the region on the same tick and capture progress must stop or reverse.
The owning team disconnects or changes alliance while income is being awarded.
A region is captured while hidden under fog of war and needs partial disclosure.
A scripted lock or neutralization effect interrupts capture at the exact transfer tick.
Late-joining clients need current ownership, progress, and income state without replaying the full match.
common_bugs: Capture progress continues after the capture source dies or leaves the region.
Income is granted to the old owner for one extra tick after transfer.
UI overlays update before authority accepts the ownership transaction.
Contested state differs between server, minimap, and world marker.
Replay desyncs because capture sources are iterated in non-deterministic order.
balancing_notes: Capture pace controls whether territory rewards aggression, defense, or coordinated pushes.
High income regions should be exposed enough to create counterplay.
Connectivity rules can create deep strategy, but players need clear explanations for why income is blocked.
accessibility_notes: Show ownership and contested state through shape, text, pattern, and audio cues in addition to color.
Provide map overlays that can be paused or inspected without losing important capture information.
Keep capture progress readable for players who cannot track subtle world-space changes.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent each territory_region_id as gameplay state with owner, contender, capture progress, and income data. Resolve capture sources in deterministic order, commit ownership_transaction_id, then update UI and income systems from the committed transfer.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area nodes or map region data for capture zones, but keep authoritative ownership in a strategy state model. Emit ownership_changed and contested_state_changed signals for UI, minimap, scoring, and resource systems.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use replicated actors or components for territory regions, with server authority resolving capture and income. Store ownership_transaction_id and revealed state for replay, late join, and fog-of-war interactions.
implementation_notes.web: Update territory through a fixed-step reducer keyed by region ids and sorted capture sources. Render overlays from ownership state, and log transfer events so replays and server correction can rebuild map control.

relationships.reason: Territory control must define how ownership interacts with stale intel and hidden enemy presence.
Team objectives can score or gate victory from territory ownership state.
Controlled regions can generate income or resources for the owning player or team.

example_games.title: Company of Heroes
example_games.note: Uses territory sectors for resources and map control.

example_games.title: Civilization VI
example_games.note: Uses owned tiles and borders as core strategic territory.
